content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agents can repurpose the original computer vision MemoryBank’s slow-updating instance feature queue structure to solve the longstanding working memory capacity bottleneck for open-ended interactive tasks, where only high-information surprise memory traces (selected via active curiosity drives) are retained in the fast-access working set while older relevant traces are stored in the gradient-annealed MemoryBank queue. This creates a hierarchical memory system that adapts memory retention to the agent’s active learning needs rather than enforcing a fixed hard capacity limit.
